name: Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop
name_original: Caboalles de Abajo a Puerto Leitariegos — circular desde Cangas del Narcea
description: This difficult 60.8-mile touring cycling loop from Cangas del Narcea climbs 6209 feet to Puerto de Leitariegos, offering rewarding mountain
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cangas del Narcea navigate a landscape characterized by extensive natural parks, mountainous terrain, and lush forests. The region, largely encompassed by the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña e Ibias Natural Park, features significant elevation changes and well-preserved native woodlands. Cyclists encounter a blend of steep ascents, river valleys, and winding roads through oak and beech forests. The area offers diverse terrain for touring cyclists, from challenging mountain passes to quieter routes. Best touring cycling routes in Cangas Del Narcea The most popular touring cycling route is El Pozu de las Mul.leres Muertas to San Antolin de Ibias to El Coniu to La Venta — circular from La Riela, a 56.0 miles (90.2 km) trail that takes 6 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes the challenging Puerto del Connio, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Caboalles de Abajo to Puerto Leitariegos — circular from Cangas del Narcea, a difficult 60.8 miles (97.9 km) path. This route ascends to Puerto de Leitariegos, known for its good road surface and quiet traffic. Local touring cyclists also love the Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Acebo desde Cangas del Narcea — circular por el Alto de Biescas, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain to the iconic Alto del Acebo, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Cangas Del Narcea is defined by mountainous terrain, dense oak and beech forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to more accessible routes. The routes in Cangas Del Narcea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 114 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cangas Del Narcea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cangas del Narcea?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes in Cangas del Narcea, offering a wide range of experiences. Most of these routes are considered difficult due to the mountainous terrain, but there are also a few moderate and easy options available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Cangas del Narcea is characterized by diverse and often challenging terrain. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, winding roads through extensive oak and beech forests, and picturesque river valleys. The region is part of the Cantabrian Mountains, so expect a mix of steep ascents and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Cangas del Narcea suitable for beginners?
A: While Cangas del Narcea is known for its challenging routes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route and one moderate option. The majority of the 23 available routes are classified as difficult, so beginners should carefully check the route details for elevation and distance before setting out.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña, and Ibias Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. You might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas Pass&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Connio Pass&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6136851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enramada Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to the vast Muniellos Forest, though access is restricted to preserve its pristine condition.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots on the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer breathtaking views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5371946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Leitariegos&lt;/a&gt; and the Alto del Acebo are iconic cycling destinations known for their panoramic vistas. The Cueto de Arbas mountain also features a spectacular glacial lake and offers expansive views, especially when accessed via the Brañas/Leitariegos pass.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Cangas del Narcea benefits from a benevolent microclimate, making spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the colder, potentially snowy conditions of winter in the higher mountain passes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging route from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1370425610/caboalles-de-abajo-to-puerto-leitariegos-circular-from-cangas-del-narcea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caboalles de Abajo to Puerto Leitariegos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a long, steady climb with good road surfaces. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368853654/cangas-del-narcea-to-puelu-circular-via-the-paseo-courias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas del Narcea to Puelu — circular via the Paseo Courias&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Yes, the town of Cangas del Narcea itself offers various amenities, including accommodation, cafes, and restaurants. Along some routes, particularly those passing through villages or near popular passes like Leitariegos, you may find smaller establishments. The region's unique viticulture also means some wineries offer visits, complementing a Wine Museum in Santiso.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Cangas del Narcea?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's well-preserved natural landscapes, the challenge of its mountain passes like the Alto del Acebo, and the quiet roads that allow for an immersive experience in the Asturian wilderness.

Q: Are there any routes that include cultural or historical sites?
A: Beyond nature, Cangas del Narcea offers a rich cultural heritage. Cyclists can explore the town of Cangas del Narcea with its medieval, Renaissance, and Baroque architecture, or visit the impressive Monastery of Corias, often called the 'Escorial of Asturias'. Some routes might pass through charming villages showcasing ancient traditions.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the touring cycling routes in Cangas del Narcea?
A: Due to the region's relatively isolated and mountainous nature, direct public transport options to the starting points of many specific cycling routes might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Cangas del Narcea town, and then plan your cycling route from there, or arrange private transport.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes?
A: In the main town of Cangas del Narcea, you will find parking facilities. For routes starting in smaller villages or more remote areas, parking might be available in designated village car parks or along wider road shoulders. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct local access or private property.
tips: Cangas del Narcea is the largest and oldest municipality in Asturias. It has numerous wineries, a Wine Museum and a very interesting historic quarter. The Monasterio de Corias is worth seeing - it is now converted into a ''National Parador''.
The emblematic Roman bridge crosses the Luiña river, serving as a passage between the town and the Ambasaguas or Entrambasaguas neighborhood, documented since the 14th century.
Caboalles de Abajo is a small and beautiful mountain town between Caboalles de Arriba and Villablino. There is a beautiful greenway that connects the three towns called the Laciana Greenway.
One of the longest climbs in the area. Mountain pass village at +1500m height with great restaurant, ski station and glacier lagoon

This is a great place to visit if you want to learn more about wine in this region. You will discover the origins, history and other interesting facts. The price to enter is less than €2 and for a little more you can get a guided tour.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for the Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop, and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts and ends in Cangas del Narcea. As a larger town, you should be able to find suitable parking within the town before beginning your ride. The route passes through villages in the initial kilometers, so parking might be available in various spots.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a difficult touring bicycle route, demanding very good fitness. It features significant and sustained climbs, such as the ascent to Puerto de Leitariegos, which can be over 20 km long with more than 1000 meters of elevation gain and average gradients around 4-4.5%. Be prepared for a demanding ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route predominantly features well-paved road surfaces, making it suitable for road cycling. While the main climb to Puerto de Leitariegos is generally well-maintained, some broader routes in the Cangas del Narcea area might include unpaved or more difficult segments, though this is less likely on the primary Leitariegos climb. Traffic is minimal after passing through initial villages like Bimeda.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle the Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is generally during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During winter, Puerto de Leitariegos can be affected by snow and ice, as it hosts a small ski resort, making cycling difficult or impossible. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or scenic points along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll start in Cangas del Narcea, where you can see the Roman Bridge of Cangas del Narcea and potentially visit the Cangas Wine Museum. The route also passes through Caboalles de Abajo. The summit of Puerto de Leitariegos is a significant landmark, marking a border point and offering views, though some find the scenery during the climb itself more enjoyable for the views behind you.

faqs.question: Does this cycling loop pass through any protected natural areas, and are there any permits or fees required?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area is part of the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña e Ibias Natural Park. While there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on the main roads through the park, it's important to respect the natural environment and any regulations regarding wildlife or protected areas. This park is known for its native forests and biodiversity, including brown bears.

faqs.question: Is the Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling loop. While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on the public roads, it's generally not considered a 'dog-friendly' trail in the traditional sense for hiking. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is accustomed to long rides in a bike trailer or can keep pace safely on a leash if you stop, and always adhere to local leash laws and clean-up regulations, especially within the Natural Park.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Fuentes del Narcea Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña e Ibias Natural Park is renowned for its rich biodiversity. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, and the park is a significant habitat for brown bears. Although direct encounters are rare, it's a good reminder of the wild nature of the region. You'll also pass through extensive native forests.

faqs.question: Does the Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. Notably, about 21% of the loop covers part of the [CIMA AS26] Leitariegos * Cangas del Narcea / Bimeda route, and approximately 14% is part of the Transcantábrica. Smaller segments also touch upon [CIMA AS26] Leitariegos * Sonande and Calle Velarde.

faqs.question: Are there any specific considerations for traffic on this route?
faqs.answer: In the initial kilometers, as the route passes through villages like Cangas del Narcea and Bimeda, you may encounter some traffic. However, after these initial sections, the route typically becomes very quiet, offering a more serene cycling experience as you ascend towards Puerto de Leitariegos.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for cycling the Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop?
faqs.answer: While the climb to Puerto de Leitariegos is long and steady, some cyclists note that the most picturesque views are often behind you as you ascend. Make sure to occasionally look back and appreciate the unfolding Asturian landscape. Also, consider starting early to avoid any potential afternoon winds or heat, especially during summer months.

waypoints.name: Roman Bridge of Cangas del Narcea
